data.title: SentinelOne remains undervalued despite transition to AI-native security platform: Citron
data.description:
SentinelOne (S) remains undervalued following its transition from an endpoint security vendor to an AI-native data and security platform, according to Citron Research.

SentinelOne shares have declined more than 30% year to date.

Citron set a $32 price target on the stock, highlighting its third-quarter fiscal 2026 financial results and its recent involvement in helping crack the Salt Typhoon hacking case.

Salt Typhoon was a Chinese-government-linked hacker group that made waves when it broke into multiple global telecommunication company networks in September 2024. T-Mobile (TMUS), Verizon (VZ), AT&T (T) Charter Communications (CHTR), Fortinet (FTNT) and Cisco (CSCO) were among those affected. One of the biggest headlines from the event was the targeting of personal phone data on U.S. President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance during the election campaign.

"This week, SentinelLabs broke the most significant attribution story in the Salt Typhoon investigation — the largest telecom breach in U.S. history," said Citron Research in an investor report. "Their researcher traced two Chinese state hackers back to Cisco's own training program. Wired picked it up. National security circles are buzzing."

"The irony? Salt Typhoon exploited vulnerabilities in Cisco, Palo Alto (PANW), and Fortinet (FTNT) devices — the legacy infrastructure incumbents that trade at premium multiples while SentinelOne trades at 5x sales like it's going out of business," Citron added.

Citron also noted that SentinelOne's emerging products, such as cloud, data and AI, accounted for half of its new contract value in its latest earnings results. The firm highlighted its Purple AI platform.

"Purple AI is the industry's most advanced generative AI security analyst, and it represents SentinelOne's clearest competitive moat," Citron said. "Unlike competitors bolting AI features onto legacy architectures, Purple AI was built natively into the Singularity Platform from inception."

A recent IDC study found that Purple AI presents a 338% three-year return on investment, 55% faster threat remediation and a 60% reduced chance of major security events.

"SentinelOne is priced like BlackBerry (BB) but growing like CrowdStrike (CRWD)," Citron added. "That disconnect creates one of the most compelling risk/reward opportunities in software."

data.title: Rivian develops AI chip for autonomous driving, unveils Autonomy+
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (RIVN) revealed a new custom chip and artificial intelligence models to expand its autonomous driving ambitions at the company’s Autonomy and AI Day  in Palo Alto, California.

At the event, the EV manufacturer also said it plans to offer an Autonomy+ subscription providing hands-free driving for an upfront purchase of $2,500 or $49.99 per month, a more competitive offering compared to Tesla’s (TSLA) $99 per month subscription.

New models will come equipped with lidar sensors, Rivian’s new custom chip (which will replace Nvidia and Qualcomm chips), and an “autonomy computer” to allow for Level 4 self-driving capabilities, underscoring the company’s robotaxi ambitions.

SAE Level 4, or high automation driving, is defined as one in which the car can handle all driving tasks within specific conditions without human intervention, though not considered “full self-driving.” 

By comparison, Tesla (TSLA) vehicles are SAE Level 2, requiring driver supervision and readiness to take over.

Rivian (RIVN) shares are down more than 3% but are off their session lows as a result of the recent development.

data.title: Corbus declines following early-stage results on oral obesity asset CRB-913
data.description:
• Corbus (CRBP) is off ~3% in Thursday trading after reporting data from its phase 1 a single ascending dose and multiple ascending dose study of CRB-913, its oral candidate for chronic obesity.
  • The SAD portion included eight cohorts that received ascending doses of CRB-913 up to a maximum of 600 mg/day or placebo. The MAD portion had four cohorts who received ascending doses of 25 mg, 75 mg or 150 mg daily or placebo. Only one of the cohorts in each portion enrolled obese patients
  • The MAD cohort of obese patients achieved a mean placebo-adjusted weight loss of 2.9% by day 14. Efficacy results were not disclosed for the SAD portion.
  • Corbus noted that drug was well tolerated with no serious treatment-emergent adverse events reported in any of the cohorts, nor any reports of nausea, vomiting, or constipation. However, there were three events of mild anxiety and one of mild irritability in the obese MAD cohort at 150 mg/day, though they were short-lived and didn't require medical intervention.

data.title: Charter Communications most shorted S&P 500 communications stocks in November; Alphabet least shorted
data.description:
Bets against communication services stocks rose in November compared to the end of October, with Charter Communications (CHTR) being the most shorted stock in the sector and Google-parent Alphabet (GOOG) taking the spot for the least shorted.

Average short interest across S&P 500 communications services stocks was 2% of shares float at the end of November, up from 1.83% in October.

The S&P 500’s communications services sector (XLC) is up nearly 21% so far this year, compared to the 17% rise in the broader market.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

 

Meta Platforms Class A (META), Alphabet Class A (GOOGL) and Netflix (NFLX), the three largest contributors to the index, had short interest of 1.49%, 1.37%, and 1.89%, respectively.

Charter Communications (CHTR) had 16.96 million shares short as on Nov-end, or 19.05% of shares float.

TKO Group (TKO) was the second most shorted stock at 11.08% of shares float, followed by Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) and The Trade Desk (TTD), with short interest of 10.75% and 10.60%, respectively.

Alphabet (GOOG) was the least shorted stock, having 41.67 million shares sold.

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

 

 

Media stocks were the most shorted industry within the sector, with 3.59% short interest as of end-November, up from 3.58% during end-October.

Wireless Telecommunication Services took the second spot with 3.11% short interest as of November, compared to 2.57% at October-end.

Interactive Media and Services, which has stocks like Alphabet and Meta Platforms, was the least shorted industry in the sector, with 1.22% short interest as of November.

data.title: Analysts expect the Altria CEO transition to be seamless
data.description:
Altria (MO) traded slightly higher in Thursday trading after announcing that CEO Billy Gifford will be retiring and current CFO Salvatore Mancuso will succeed him. The CEO switch will be effective on May 14, 2026. In addition, the board elected Heather Newman as CFO.

Goldman Sachs analyst Bonnie Herzog said she viewed the announcement positively on the expectation for a seamless transition and limited disruption of Altria's (MO) ongoing transformation of its vision to Move Beyond Smoking and its 2028 Enterprise Goals, even if the leadership announcement was a little earlier than expected. "Mancuso's appointment as CEO was a result of MO's long-term succession planning process, including the assessment of multiple internal and external candidates," updated Herzog. She added that during Gifford's tenure at Altria (MO) he demonstrated strong leadership capabilities and a thoughtful and strategic approach to managing the business.

Bank of America analyst Lisa Lewandowski noted that Mancuso and Newman are known to investors, and the firm expects the transition to be smooth. "We would not expect any changes in mgmt’s commitment to shareholder returns," advised Lewandowski.

Shares of Altria (MO) were up 1.0% in early afternoon trading to $59.27 vs. the 52-week range of $50.08 to $68.60. The dividend yield for new buyers of the stock is 7.2%.

data.title: Dow climbs and the Nasdaq slides after Oracle’s post-earnings drop
data.description:
Wall Street traded mixed on Thursday as the tech-oriented Nasdaq Composite dragged lower after Oracle (ORCL) posted mixed results, pressuring the AI trade.

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slid lower by 0.6%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was near even while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) climbed by 1.2%.

Across sectors, nine of the 11 S&P groups were in positive territory, led by Materials. At the other end of the spectrum, Info Tech has been the weakest performer so far Thursday.

Investors also continue to react to Wednesday’s Fed decision to cut its policy rate by 25 basis points to 3.50%-3.75%, which is now its lowest level in three years. 

“The Federal Reserve sent a clear message that the Fed is in the midst of an accommodative phase, largely driven by the recent weakness in the labor market. While 2026 may not bring as many rate cuts as 2025, it's clear that the Fed's prior hiking cycle that disrupted markets and the economy post-Covid is firmly in the rearview mirror, and that's bullish for stocks,” Chris Kampitsis, managing partner of Barnum Financial Group said. 

Furthermore, Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid stated: “Bond yields have also moved lower, which partly reflects the Fed and the wider risk-off tone this morning.” Reid noted Japan’s robust bond auction and Australia’s unexpected employment contraction as contributing factors. 

Yields were lower across the curve, with the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) falling 3 basis points to 3.51% and the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dropping 4 basis points to 4.11%. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Oracle (ORCL) dropped 12.7% while shares of Mosaic (MOS) pushed up by 7.9%.

data.title: Rhythm advances lead asset into late-stage trial in Prader-Willi syndrome
data.description:
Rhythm Pharma (RYTM) added ~15% on Thursday after the company announced plans to advance its lead asset, setmelanotide, into a registrational Phase 3 trial for a rare genetic disorder called Prader-Willi syndrome following a promising mid-stage readout.

Characterized by an extreme sense of hunger (hyperphagia), PWS marks a major unmet medical need affecting an estimated 20,000 Americans and about 40,000 globally, with limited therapeutic options.

Citing initial data from an open-label Phase 2 trial of 18 PWS patients, the Boston, Massachusetts-based biotech said that setmelanotide caused potential therapeutic effects with reductions in BMI and hyperphagia at Months 3 and 6.

Setmelanotide, currently available in the U.S. as Imcivree for several other obesity-related rare disorders, indicated a safety and tolerability profile consistent with prior studies, Rhythm (RYTM) added.

“We are encouraged by these preliminary results, which give us confidence to advance setmelanotide into a registrational Phase 3 trial for PWS,” CEO David Meeker noted.

The company plans to advance setmelanotide into the Phase 3 registrational trial, subject to the successful completion of the ongoing Phase 2 trial, from which additional data are expected in H1 2026.

data.title: Robinhood stock drops after disappointing November trading volumes
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (HOOD) shares gapped down 8.3% in Thursday afternoon trading after the company posted markedly lower trading volumes across most asset classes sequentially in November.

The biggest M/M declines were in equity (-37%) and options (-28%) trading. Crypto volumes were also decidedly lower, although events trading swelled last month.

“With equity and crypto markets declining during the month of November, assets under custody declined 6.1% to $325bn, which was the first m/m decline since February," Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Brett Knoblauch, who ranks HOOD as Overweight, wrote in a note to clients.

November was a weaker month for Robinhood (HOOD), though "we believe it was another solid quarter," he added, noting previous months had set new records.

data.title: Mortgage rates edge up this week but remain below the year-to-date average
data.description:
Mortgage rates inched up this week, but remained well below the year-to-date average, the latest Freddie Mac (FMCC) Primary Mortgage Survey showed.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.22% as of December 11, up from 6.19% last week and down from 6.60% in the same period a year ago.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.54%, compared to 5.44% a week ago and 5.84% last year.

"The average 30-year fixed-rate mortgage is well below the year-to-date average of 6.62%, providing some sense of balance to the housing market," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.

data.title: Starbucks worker's union stages 'Downtown Throwdown' in Chicago - update
data.description:
Updated 12:11ET with comment from Starbucks spokesperson regarding the SWU strike.

The Starbucks Workers United ((SWU)) strike continues to grind on with the action escalating to a 48-hour demonstration at the company’s locations across Chicago.

The “Downtown Throwdown” will spread to over a dozen restaurants located in Chicago’s Loop and River North, including Chicago’s Reserve Roastery, the world’s largest Starbucks. Demonstrations will run through the day and night until Friday morning in what the union characterizes as “the boldest demonstrations yet of union baristas’ resolve.”

The “Red Cup Rebellion” kicked off mid-November to coincide with the company’s busiest day of the year, spreading to 95 stores in 65 cities within a week. The strike has since included 3,000 baristas across more than 145 stores in 105 cities, making this the longest, nationwide unfair labor practice strike in the company’s history.

The strike has also drawn solidarity from trade unions across the globe as the “No Contract, No Coffee” campaign resulted in demonstrations in South America, Europe and Asia.

In the company’s hometown, three stores currently remain closed due to staffing shortages resulting from the strike, including the iconic Starbucks Reserve Roastery in Seattle’s Capitol Hill neighborhood. While the company states the store has been closed since September as part of a major corporate restructuring and store consolidation, pressure from the union likely contributed to the decision to permanently shutter the restaurant.

To end the action, the union is demanding Starbucks (SBUX) offer employees a new contract that would provide for better hours to improve staffing, higher take-home pay, and a resolution of unfair labor practices by the company.

“Finalizing a fair union contract would cost Starbucks less than one average day’s sales and less than Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol’s $96 million compensation for just four months of work in 2024,” the union said.

Although the SWU says the company has not attempted to negotiate an end to the strike, Starbucks (SBUX) claims the union walked away from talks while also arguing that it already provides top retail employment with an average hourly compensation exceeding $30 in pay and benefits.

Despite attempts to disrupt the business and put a meaningful dent in sales, the strike has so far done seemingly little financial harm to Starbucks (SBUX). This year’s Red Cup Day was the strongest in the company’s history in terms of sales and store traffic., and management maintains that the strikes haven’t significantly disrupted operations nor impacted the company’s bottom-line.

"As we’ve said, 99% of our 17,000 U.S. locations remain open and welcoming customers —including many the union publicly stated would strike but never closed or have since reopened. Regardless of the union’s plans, we do not anticipate any meaningful disruption. When the union is ready to return to the bargaining table, we’re ready to talk," said Starbucks spokesperson Jaci Anderson to Seeking Alpha. 

"Starbucks offers the best job in retail. People choose to work here and stay here—our turnover is less than half the industry average, and we receive more than a million job applications every year," she added.

During the first week of the strike, only 49 coffeehouses experienced any disruption and 29 have already reopened. Additionally, the company stated that less than 1% of all coffeehouses are experiencing any level of disruption from SWU, who represent less than 4% of the company's workforce, the company said.
